identity.name: Meera Iyer
identity.headline: Senior Software Engineer | Backend & Distributed Systems

summary.short: Backend engineer who keeps payment systems fast and cheap under load — eight years on distributed systems in Go and Rust.
summary.long: Senior software engineer working on the backend of high-traffic payment and messaging systems. Comfortable owning a service from the design doc through the on-call pager, and happiest when a rewrite drops both the latency graph and the cloud bill. Maintains a small open-source rate limiter that other teams now depend on.

current_roles.title: Senior Software Engineer
highlights: Rebuilt the payments service to handle 12,000 requests a second, cutting p99 latency from 800ms to 90ms.
Led the migration off a monolith into six services and shipped it over a weekend with no downtime.
Wrote the rate limiter that now guards every public API and open-sourced it at 600 GitHub stars.
Trimmed the AWS bill 30% by moving batch jobs to spot instances and right-sizing the fleet.
Set up the on-call rotation and runbooks that dropped mean time to recovery from 45 to 12 minutes.

current_roles.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Built the driver-location pipeline processing 2 billion events a day on Kafka.
Cut a nightly reconciliation job from six hours to twenty minutes by rewriting it in Go.

projects.name: ratekeeper
projects.description: Open-source token-bucket rate limiter for Go services, used in production by three teams.

projects.name: pgslice
projects.description: CLI that shards a Postgres table online without a maintenance window.

achievements: Cut payment p99 latency almost 9x on the same hardware through a service rewrite.
Open-sourced a rate limiter three internal teams and a handful of outside ones now run.
Halved the platform's on-call load by fixing the three alerts that caused most of the pages.
Gave a talk on zero-downtime migrations at Rootconf 2023.
